title: Sonnet - My Heart Shall Be Thy Garden
content:
My heart shall be thy garden. Come, my own,
        Into thy garden; thine be happy hours
        Among my fairest thoughts, my tallest flowers,
    From root to crowning petal, thine alone.

    Thine is the place from where the seeds are sown
        Up to the sky enclosed, with all its showers.
        But ah, the birds, the birds! Who shall build bowers
    To keep these thine? O friend, the birds have flown.

    For as these come and go, and quit our pine
        To follow the sweet season, or, new-comers,
            Sing one song only from our alder-trees.

    My heart has thoughts, which, though thine eyes hold mine,
        Flit to the silent world and other summers,
         With wings that dip beyond the silver seas.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Alice Christiana Gertrude Thompson Meynell, titled "Sonnet - My Heart Shall Be Thy Garden", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
